description: Verification checklist defining all checks the Verifier agent performs on every document. Each check has an ID, category, description, severity on failure, and evaluation method.

checks.name: structure_complete
checks.description: The document's overall structure is complete: it has valid front matter, a proper heading hierarchy, and conforms to the doc type specification.

criteria: Front matter block exists and is valid YAML
H1 or title field is present
At least one H2 section exists
Heading levels do not skip (e.g., no H4 directly under H2)

checks.name: all_sections_present
checks.description: Every required section defined in the documentation plan is present in the document. Section matching is case-insensitive with minor variation tolerance.

criteria: Extract all H2 headings from the document
Compare against plan.sections where required=true
Allow fuzzy matching (Levenshtein distance ≤ 3 or substring match)
Report each missing required section individually

checks.name: citations_valid
checks.description: All inline citations reference source_ids that exist in the evidence pack. No orphaned or fabricated citation references.

criteria: Extract all [Evidence: src-XXX] patterns from the document
Look up each src-XXX in evidence_pack.sources[].source_id
Flag any citation where the source_id is not found
A single fabricated citation is a critical finding

checks.name: links_working
checks.description: All URLs in the document are accessible and return successful HTTP responses. Internal cross-references resolve to valid targets.

criteria: Extract all http/https URLs from the document
Navigate to each URL (max 30, sample if more)
Accept HTTP 200 and 3xx redirects that resolve to 200
Flag 4xx, 5xx, and unreachable URLs
Check internal anchor links (#heading-id) resolve within the document

checks.name: no_fabricated_claims
checks.description: For a sample of cited claims, verify that the document's statement is supported by the actual evidence source content. Detect misrepresentation, exaggeration, or contradiction.

criteria: Select up to 20 citations for deep verification
Extract the claim text surrounding each citation
Retrieve the cited source content from the evidence pack
Compare claim to source using semantic comparison
Flag mismatches as 'major', contradictions as 'critical'

checks.name: terminology_consistent
checks.description: Technical terms and proper nouns are used consistently throughout the document. The same concept should not be referred to by different names without explicit definition.

criteria: Build a term frequency map across the document
Identify potential synonyms or variants referring to the same concept
Cross-reference against the organizational terminology database
Flag inconsistencies where two terms appear to reference the same thing

checks.name: audience_appropriate
checks.description: The document's language complexity, assumed knowledge, and explanation depth are appropriate for the target audience defined in the plan.

criteria: Read the plan's audience.technical_level
For 'novice': check that technical terms are defined, concepts are explained
For 'intermediate': check that domain basics are assumed but specialized concepts are introduced
For 'advanced'/'expert': check that unnecessary explanation is minimal
Flag unexplained jargon for novice audiences
Flag over-explanation for expert audiences

checks.name: formatting_correct
checks.description: The document follows Markdown formatting standards: consistent heading levels, proper code blocks with language identifiers, well-formed tables, and clean link syntax.

criteria: Code blocks use fenced syntax with language identifiers
Tables have header rows and consistent column counts
No raw HTML (unless doc type permits)
No broken Markdown syntax (unclosed bold/italic, malformed links)
Lists use consistent markers (all bullets or all numbers, not mixed)
Images have alt text

checks.name: front_matter_complete
checks.description: The YAML front matter contains all required fields as defined by the doc type specification and the documentation plan.

criteria: Parse YAML front matter block
Check all required fields from doc-types.json for the specified doc_type
Check all required fields from plan.front_matter.fields
Verify non-empty values for required fields
Validate date fields are ISO-8601 format
Verify status field is 'draft'

checks.name: confidence_above_threshold
checks.description: The overall weighted confidence score meets or exceeds the pass threshold of 70. This is the final gating check after all other checks have been evaluated.

criteria: Calculate weighted scores for all categories
Sum to produce overall confidence score (0-100)
Score >= 70 passes this check
Score < 70 fails this check regardless of individual category scores
Any critical finding in other checks forces this to fail

critical.description: Fundamental deficiency that makes the document unfit for purpose. Triggers automatic FAIL regardless of overall score.

major.description: Significant issue that materially reduces document quality or accuracy. Multiple major issues can push the score below threshold.
major.score_impact: -15 to -30 points from category score per finding.

minor.description: Quality issue that should be fixed but doesn't undermine the document's core value. Unlikely to cause a FAIL alone.
minor.score_impact: -5 to -10 points from category score per finding.

info.description: Observation or suggestion for improvement. Does not affect score. Included for human reviewer awareness.
